mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects consumer demand strength that lifts GDP forecasts and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as real yields adjust higher.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of RSAFS monthly growth vs 20-day forward yield change remains below 0.03, or the top tercile of RSAFS growth fails to produce higher future yields than the bottom tercile by at least 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and triggers market expectations of Fed rate hikes, raising short-term yields and pulling up the 10-year yield.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% within a one-month window
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE first drops below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Together, high CPI inflation and a very low federal funds rate produce deeply negative real short-term rates, forcing the market to price in future tightening that pushes the 10-year yield upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Yields fail to rise in at least 60% of triggered occurrences, or the rank IC of the interaction variable (real rate) against forward yield change is below 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In the initial phase of a monetary tightening cycle, after the first rate hike from a low base, the 10-year yield rises as the market fully prices in a sequence of further hikes and stronger growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points within a month, breaking a period of stability
regime_hint: initial phase of monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of such first-hike episodes, or the cumulative 60-day yield change is negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job creation (large payroll increases) combined with rising housing starts signals broad economic strength and demand for credit, lifting inflation expectations and pushing the 10-year yield higher.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > +200,000 and HOUST annualized rate > 1,000,000 units
failure_condition: Yields fall in more than 50% of jointly triggered events, or the interaction-weighted composite fails to produce a positive average yield change over the next 20 days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates expanding output capacity and strong aggregate demand, leading to higher inflation premia and a structural rise in the 10-year yield.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year IC of INDPRO monthly growth vs 60-day forward yield change is below 0.02, or the top quartile of INDPRO growth does not consistently precede yield increases.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1,000,000 (annualized) signals robust residential investment and mortgage demand, which pushes upward pressure on long-term yields through increased bond supply and higher expected short-term rates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST annualized rate exceeds 1,000,000 units in a given month
failure_condition: In more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1M, the 20-day forward yield change is negative, or the historical hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index level is above its trailing 12-month moving average, inflation expectations are rising, which forces bond investors to demand higher compensation, lifting the 10-year yield.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL current level > 12-month simple moving average of CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: Yields do not rise in at least 55% of months when CPI exceeds its 12-month MA, or the average forward yield change is not positive across such months.
